academic.passage: Photosynthesis is the process through which green plants and some other organisms convert light energy into chemical energy, specifically glucose. This occurs primarily in the chloroplasts of plant cells. The overall reaction involves taking in carbon dioxide from the air and water from the soil, producing glucose and releasing oxygen as a byproduct. This process is vital for life on Earth, as it provides the primary energy source for nearly all ecosystems and contributes to the atmospheric oxygen essential for respiration in animals.

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Photosynthesis is essential for life on Earth.
Chloroplasts are found in animal cells.
Carbon dioxide is harmful to plants.
Oxygen is not important for living organisms.
questions.explain: 這段文字的主要思想是光合作用對地球生命至關重要。

questions.q: What do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.explain: 植物在光合作用過程中吸收二氧化碳和水。

questions.q: What can be inferred about the role of chlorophyll?
options: It is not involved in photosynthesis.
questions.explain: 這段文字暗示葉綠素的角色是捕獲光能。

questions.q: What does the term 'byproduct' refer to in the context of photosynthesis?
questions.explain: 在光合作用中，'副產品'指的是二次產生的產品，即氧氣。

daily.passage: Attention students! The library will be closed this Saturday for maintenance. Please plan your study sessions accordingly. Normal hours will resume on Monday. Thank you for your understanding!

questions.q: When will the library be closed?
questions.explain: 圖書館將在本週六關閉。

questions.q: When will normal library hours resume?
questions.explain: 正常的圖書館開放時間將在下星期一恢復。
